questions.question: Consider a feature space with two classes of patterns: a circle and a square. Suppose you have a set of training data where a circle is approximated by a set of prototypes (e.g., { (1,1) }), and a square is approximated by another set of prototypes (e.g., { (2,2) }). If a test pattern is { (1.5,1.5) }, which class will the Minimum Distance Classifier assign it to, and why?

Given a feature space with a set of prototype patterns for two classes {X, Y} and the distance formula d(p, q) = sqrt((x1 - x2)^2 + (y1 - y2)^2), where p and q represent any two points in the feature space, determine the minimum distance between a test pattern and the prototypes of a class.

Suppose we have a binary image with two classes (e.g., foreground and background) and we want to use a Minimum Distance Classifier to classify pixels based on their feature vectors. If we want to obtain a certain degree of robustness to noise in the feature vectors, what approach would you take, and why?
